title: % Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
Numbers placed along the marked line and circles must no repeat. The same set of 8 numbers is used along the circles.

title: 0 to 9 Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, the range of digits is now 0-9. Some cells have been split with a diagonal, and two digits go into those cells.

title: 0-8 Arrow Sudoku
instruction:
Place a digit from 0-8 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and 3x3 box contains each digit exactly once.
Each digit placed in a cell with a circle must be the sum of the digits placed in the cells that the adjoining arrow passes through. Digits may repeat on arrows.

title: 0-8 Skyscrapers Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
Each number from 1-8 represents the height of the building. The clues outside the grid indicate the number of buildings visible from the corresponding direction. A taller building will hide any shorter buildings behind it.
0 does not represent a building, and does not count towards the number of buildings in a corresponding direction.

title: 24-Trio Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
The three numbers placed along marked lines must result in 24 after applying two of the arithmetic operations (+, x, -, /). The order of these numbers along the line does not matter.
E.g. 395 can be combined as 24 = (3 x 5) + 9.

title: 3D Sudoku
instruction:
Place a digit from 1-8 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and outlined regions contains each digit exactly once.
This puzzle has 12 lines (instead of rows and columns) and 6 regions. The lines are not straight. The way to identify a line is by taking a cell at the edge of the grid and to follow a path by finding the next cell that touches the opposite side of the cell. You repeat this process until you reach the other edge.

title: All Odd/Even Sudoku
instruction: Apply Classic Sudoku rules. Additionally, within each region, the digits in the grey cells are either all odd or all even.

title: Almost Palindrome Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Numbers formed by the digits on the gray lines are "almost palindromes", i.e. they can be read equally in both directions except for exactly one couple of cells that do not match on each line.

title: Alternating Stripes Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Along each grey stripe, digits must be alternately larger and smaller than their immediate neighbours. Digits may not repeat anywhere on a stripe.

title: Anti-Consecutive Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent cells marked with X cannot contain digits which are consecutive to each other. Not all such adjacent cells are marked. In other words, adjacent cells with no marking may or may not contain consecutive digits.

title: Anti-Diagonal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each marked diagonal must contain only 3 different digits.

title: Anti-Knight Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits placed in cells connected by a chess Knight's move must be different.
otherNames: Antiknight Sudoku, No Knight Step Sudoku

title: Arrow Sequences Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, there are circles and arrows in the grid. For a digit N in a circle, the digit N+1 is at the end of the attached arrow. The digits along the arrow (not including the circle) must form a sequence of consecutive digits. (The digit in the circle can appear along the arrow itself.)

title: Arrow Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each digit placed in a cell with a circle must be the sum of the digits placed in the cells that the adjoining arrow passes through. Digits may repeat on arrows.

title: Ascending Starters Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the first set of all ascending digits, starting from the first cell from the corresponding side. Sets may consist of only one digit.

title: Average Arrows Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The number placed in a cell with a circle must be the arithmetic mean of the digits along the path of each arrow. Numbers may repeat on an arrow.

title: Battenburg Little Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Wherever 2 odd and 2 even digits form a 2x2 chessboard pattern, a Battenburg marking is given. If there is no marking, the above pattern is not allowed. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the digits contained in the cells in the direction of the corresponding arrow.

title: Battenburg Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Everywhere 2 odd and 2 even digits form a 2x2 chessboard pattern, a Battenburg marking is given. If there is no marking, the above pattern is not allowed.

title: Battleships Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, place the given set of battleships into the grid; ships cannot touch even diagonally. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the digits occupied by ships in the corresponding row or column.

title: Before 1 - After 9 Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, numbers outside the grid indicate the sum of all digits before the digit 1 in that row/column or after the digit 9 in that row/column, going from left to right and from top to bottom.

title: Before 9 Sudoku
instruction: Apply Classic Sudoku rules. Additionally, the clues outside the grid show the sum of the digits that appear before 9 in the corresponding row or column.

title: Between Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Every digit on a grey line must have a value which lies between the values of the two digits in the circles at the ends of the line. Digits may repeat on a line.

title: Big Bands Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each of the grey twisty bands must contain one of the given 7-digit numbers. The numbers can be entered in either of two directions.

title: Big-Small Japanese Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the sums of groups in the given order. Clues on top refer to groups of big digits (5-9). These groups are separated by at least one small digit (1-4). Clues on the left refer to groups of small digits, that are separated by at least one big digit.

title: Block Sum Relations Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The given signs show the relations between the sums of the digits of the two sides. For the example: 147>235 because 1+4+7=12 > 2+3+5=10. The 3x3 boxes are separated slightly for better clarity.

title: Bouncing X-Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the first X digits along the indicated diagonal, where X is equal to the first digit placed in that direction. If X exceeds the number of cells in the direction, the sum bounces off the edge at a 90-degree angle and continues. Digits may be repeated in sums.

title: Box Sequences Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If two neighbouring cells are connected with a grey line, they contain consecutive digits. All possible sequences with length of at least 3 are marked inside each 3x3 box.

title: Braille Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The dots given inside some cells form part (or all) of the Braille representation of the digit.

title: Bust Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A number (X) outside the grid is the minimum possible value for which the sum of the first X digits in the corresponding direction is greater than 21.

title: Capsules Sudoku
instruction: Place a number from 1-8 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked region each number exactly once. Note that cells containing capsules span three columns.

title: Cave Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Symbols around the grid describe in correct order all increasing and decreasing sequences of length 3 or more that exist in the corresponding row/column. Sharp end of each symbol points where the small numbers are, open end is where the big numbers are. Number inside a symbol gives the length of a sequence.

title: Chess Kings Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Two numbers are white and black chess Kings. As in real chess, Kings can not touch other Kings, not even diagonally, even if they are not in opposite color. It is a part of solving to find which numbers are chess Kings.

title: Clock 0-8 Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 0-8 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 3x3 box contains each digit exactly once. Some digital clocks are shown. When the puzzle is completed, all clocks should display a valid time in 24-hour format (HH:MM); i.e. a clock cannot display 43:65.

title: Clock Faces Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Four digits around a white circle are placed in an increasing order starting from one of the four cells and going clockwise. Four digits around a black circle are placed in an increasing order starting from one of the four cells and going anticlockwise. All possible circles are marked.

title: Clock Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some digital clocks are shown. When the puzzle is completed, all clocks should display a valid time in 24-hour format (HH:MM); i.e. a clock cannot display 43:65.

title: Clone Fortress Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in the same place in both shaded figures must be identical. A digit placed in a shaded cell must be strictly greater than digits placed in orthogonally adjacent unshaded cells.

title: Clone or Renban Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules. Each shape in the grid appears four times. Two shapes are clones, and digits in the same place in each shape must be identical. Other two shapes are renban groups, and they must contain a set of distinct, consecutive digits in any order. Cloned shapes may or may not be renban groups at the same time, and digits in it may repeat.
Identifying which shapes are clones and which are renban groups is part of the solving.

title: Clone Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in the same place in each shaded figure must be identical.

title: Cloned Shapes Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each shape is cloned, including the digits it contains, without rotating or mirroring. The position of the digits inside each shape is fixed - the same digits have to be placed at the same position inside the identical shapes. The digit may appear more than once in a shape.

title: Cloned Strands Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each strand must have exactly the same sequence of digits. A digit can appear more than once on a single strand.

title: Coded Clone Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Two cages with a dashed border are marked by the same letter if they contain the same set of digits. For each cage marked with the same letter digits in the same place must be identical. Digits may repeat in a cage.

title: Coded pairs Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Two cages with a dashed border are marked by the same letter if they contain the same pair of digits (in arbitrary order).

title: Coded Pointing Evens Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The clues with an arrow outside the grid indicate the number of even digits in the indicated direction. However, some of these clues are replaced by letters. Different letters mean different numbers. Lettered clues can be equal to any of the given numbered clues.

title: Consecutive Chains Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in the grey areas must form a chain of consecutive digits from 1 to 9 by moving horizontally or vertically from one cell to the next.

title: Consecutive Clone Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally, in each shaded region of the same shape, the numbers placed in corresponding cells of the regions must be consecutive.

title: Consecutive Pairs Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If a circle is given between two adjacent cells, then the digits in those cells must be consecutive. Not all circles are given; adjacent cells without a circle may contain either consecutive numbers or non-consecutive numbers.

title: Consecutive Quads Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, a white circle means that the 2×2 area around it contains exactly one consecutive pair of digits. A black circle means that the 2×2 area around it contains at least two consecutive pairs of digits. Repeats are allowed (e.g., 2-4-5-6 is valid with 4-5 and 5-6, and so is 2-5-6-5). (Not all possible circles are necessarily given.)

title: Consecutive Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. In all cases where two neighbouring cells contain consecutive digits, a grey bar is placed between those cells.

title: Corner/Edge Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each speech balloon indicates C numbers which are in corner cells and E numbers which are in edge cells of the corresponding 3x3 box.

title: Count different Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Number in a circle tells how many distinct digits you can find along the corresponding arrow.

title: Count odd Sudoku
instruction: Number in a circle tells the count of odd digits in up to 10 cells touching the pair of cells with the circle.

title: Count the odd ones Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Number in a circle tells you how many odd digits you can find along the corresponding arrow.

title: Counting neighbours Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Number in a cell with a circle tells you how many distinct digits you can find in up to 8 neighbouring cells (sharing edge or corner). Number in a cell with a cross tells you how many distinct digits you can find in up to 4 diagonally neighbouring cells (sharing a corner). All possible circles and crosses are drawn.

title: Cross Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. There are four cells marked by a cross several times in the grid. Both diagonal pairs in them have the same sum. All crosses are marked.

title: Cross-Number Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Place all the listed number sequences into shaded strips. Number sequences can be entered in any of four directions.

title: Czech Outsider Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. There are some numbers with the arrows outside the grid. Each of these numbers must appear on the diagonal given by the arrow. If the same number is given multiple times for an arrow, it must appear at least as many times as it is given.

title: Dead or Alive Arrows Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. There are some white and grey arrows in the grid. The digit placed in the cell with white arrow must not repeat in the direction of the arrow. The digit placed in the cell with grey arrow is repeated at least once in the direction of the arrow.

title: Deficit Sudoku
instruction: Place a number from 1-7 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row and column contains each number exactly once, and each marked 6-cell region contains each number at most once.

title: Delimited Kropki Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Kropki dots are placed in the grid. Kropki rules are applied only for a pair of cells where the centers of cells are connected (orthogonally or diagonally) with a grey line.

title: Descriptive Pairs Sudoku
Detection Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. An arrow in a cell with digit N points to the direction where another digit N can be found. All possible arrows are drawn.

title: Determinant Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The determinants of 2x2 matrices are given in the center of the four cells. The determinant of a 2x2 matrix is a a x d - b x c, where a is the top left, b is the top right, c is bottom left and d is the bottom right element.

title: Diagonal Anti-Diagonal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. One marked diagonal must contains digits from 1-9 exactly once and the other contains exactly 3 different digits.

title: Diagonal Irregular Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 9-cell region contains each digit exactly once. Each marked diagonal must also contain each digit from 1-9 exactly once.

title: Diagonal pairs Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. There are exactly 13 pairs of cells in the grid (15 in example) satisfying the following conditions: one cell is grey, one cell is white, both contain the same digit, which is equal to the diagonal distance between the cells. There is no such pair with both cells white or both cells grey. Each digit from 1 to 8 appears at least once in a grey cell. Each cell / digit belongs to at most one pair.

title: Diagonal Skyscrapers Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each number represents the height of a building. The clues outside the grid indicate the number of buildings visible from the corresponding direction. A higher building will hide any lower buildings behind it. Each marked diagonal must also contain each number from 1-9 exactly once.

title: Diagonal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each marked diagonal must also contain each digit from 1-9 exactly once.

title: Diagonal Sum is Nine Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, all pairs of diagonally adjacent cells containing digits that sum to nine are marked by a line. (This means that all diagonally adjacent cells without a line cannot contain digits that sum to nine.)

title: Diagonal Tens Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. All diagonal pairs which sum up to 10 are marked.

title: Diagonally Consecutive Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. All pairs of diagonally adjacent cells containing consecutive digits are marked by a line. If there is no line, diagonally adjacent cells cannot contain consecutive digits.

title: Diagonally Non-Consecutive Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits placed in diagonally neighbouring cells must not be consecutive.

title: Difference 2 Neighbours Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules. Each digit in a grey cell must have at least one orthogonally adjacent digit (in eather a grey or a white cell) that is exactly 2 greater or smaller.
There may also be digits in white cells which have orthohonally adjacent digits that are exactly 2 greater or smaller.

title: Difference Sudoku
instruction: Number in a circle tells the difference between the two adjacent numbers

title: Different Around Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The small number in a two-cell cage indicates how many different digits surround the cage. Up to 10 cells touching the cage by a side or by a corner should be inspected.

title: Disguised Palindromes Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. For each grey line, there must be at least one way of removing a digit so that the remaining digits on the line form a palindrome. It is permissible that the digits on the grey line also form a palindrom before the removal step.

title: Disjoint Groups Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each number appears exactly once in each of the nine positions in 3x3 square.

title: Disparity Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1 to 9 in each empty cell so that each digit appears exactly once in every row, column, and outlined region. In addition, neighboring cells in different regions (i.e., across the bold borders) must be of opposite parity.

title: Distance to 5 Sudoku
instruction: Nine grey cells are uniquely paired to nine occurances of digit 5, the distance (vertical, horizontal or diagonal) from grey cell to digit 5 is given in the grey cell. Digit 5 cannot be placed in a grey cell.

title: Distances Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the distance (in steps) between the digits in the corresponding row or column. Digits must be placed in order of appearance.

title: Divisible by Three Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Inside each 3x3 box all sums created by three horizontally or vertically adjacent digits must be divisible by 3.

title: Divisor Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
Some clues are marked at the edges of adjacent pairs of cells. These clues must be divisors of the 2 digit numbers formed by the numbers placed in these cells, when read left-to-right or top-to-bottom.

title: Double Kropki Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent cells contianing numbers whose difference is 2 are marked with a white rhombus. Adjacent cells containing numbers whose ratio is 4 are marked with a black rhombus. Adjacent cells with no marking must not contain numbers either whose difference is 2 or whose ratio is 4.

title: Double Sandwich Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the digit(s) placed between the digits 1, 5 and 9 in the respective row or column in the correct order. The order of the digits 1,5 and 9 is not given.

title: Double Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-4 in each empty cell so that each digit appears exactly twice in each row, column, and outlined region. Identical digits cannot share an edge.

title: Dragon Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
Each 9 in the grid sees exactly 8 other cells in the 4 vertical and horizontal directions before a wall is seen. Numbers placed in each of these sets of 8 cells must not repeat.

title: Easy As Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from the given set into each of the empty cells so that each digit appears exactly once in each row, column and 3x3 outlined box. The remaining cells are blank. Each digit outside the grid is the first digit seen from the corresponding direction.

title: Either Or Sudoku
instruction: Apply standard Sudoku rules. A digit between two cells must appear in one of those 2 cells.

title: Elimination Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. If there is a digit N in a cell with an arrow, the digit N cannot apear in a direction the arrow points at.

title: Emitters Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Several cells are marked with a star. In all four directions, maximal possible length is given until the sum of the digits is higher than the value of an emitter.

title: Equal Sum Lines Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. All lines have the same sum of digits. Digits may repeat on lines.

title: Escape Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic Sudoku rules. Each of the shaded cells has an escape path to the edge of the grid. An escape path consists of a series of adjacent cells containing a descending consecutive sequence of digits. All escape paths begin with the digit in the shaded cell and end at a cell containing a 1 at the edge of the grid. Escape paths starting in different shaded cells never share any cell. It is possible that a shaded cell can have more than one escape path.

title: Even Passage Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, there must be an orthogonally-connected path of cells containing even digits that travels from the (shaded) top left corner of the grid to the (shaded) bottom right corner.

title: Even Sandwich Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The clues outside the grid indicate all digits (not necessarily in the given order) which have two even digits as immediate neighbours in the corresponding row or column. If a digit is no clued outside the grid, then its two immediate neighbours in that row or column must not both be even.

title: Even Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits placed in shaded cells must be even.

title: Expanded Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The rows and columns continue over an empty space.

title: Expression X-Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the first X digits placed in the corresponding direction, where X is equal to the first digit placed in that direction. There are different letters and mathematical expressions (consisting of only +, -, x and /) inside and outside the grid. Each letter represents a positive integer. Different letters may take on the same value.

title: Extended Extra Regions Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each marked 10-cell string must contain all digits from 1 to 9. Digits at both ends of each string must be the same.

title: Extra Large Regions Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each marked region must contain each digit from 1-9 exactly twice.

title: Extra Region Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The nine shaded cells must also contain each digit from 1-9 exactly once.

title: Extra Regions Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each of the shaded regions must also contain each digit from 1-9 exactly once.

title: Extra Space Sudoku
instruction: Place a number from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked box contains each number exactly once. The grey cells will not contain numbers.

title: Factor Lines Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. For every pair of adjacent digits on a line, one digit must be a factor/multiple of the other.

title: Faded Kropki Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent cells containing digits whose difference is 1 are marked with a white circle. Adjacent cells containing digits whose ratio is 2 are marked with a white circle too. Adjacent cells with no marking must not contain digits either whose difference is 1 or whose ratio is 2.

title: Figure Sum Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Place the given set of figures into the grid following grid lines. Figures can be rotated, but not reflected. Figures cannot touch each other even in a point. Clues show the sum of neighboring digits which are inside the figures.

title: Figures Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Dotted areas with the same shape must contain the same set of digits (but not necessarily in the same order). The digit may be repeated in area.

title: First Impression Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digit in each shaded cell is greater than the digits in the first cell of the corresponding row (leftmost cell) and the first cell of the corresponding column (topmost cell). Not all possible cells are shaded.

title: First Seen Odd/Even Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Odd clues outside the grid indicate the first odd digit from that direction. Even clues outside the grid indicate the first even digit from that direction.

title: Fives Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally, adjacent cells containing numbers whose sum or difference is 5 are marked. Adjacent cells with no marking must not contain numbers whose sum or difference is 5.

title: Flip-flop classics Sudoku
instruction:
Fill in the grid with digits 1 to 9 so that every row, column and outlined box contains nine different digits.
There are two grids to be solved on two sides of the paper. There are 12 extra squares along the grid edge. You have to bend each of those extra squares (independently) to the one or the other side to form two classic sudokus of the standard size. You should solve them after that.

title: Football Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Grey circles represent 14 football players divided into two teams. Players of each team are marked with numbers from 1 to 7. One team plays with a ball number 8, the other with a ball number 9. Player number 1 has a ball number next to him in a direction of a horizontal, vertical or diagonal pass to the player number 2. And so on up to the pass between players 6 and 7 of each team. No other player stands in the way of every pass.

title: Fortress Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A digit placed in a shaded cell must be strictly greater than digits placed in orthogonally adjacent unshaded cells.

title: Four pairs Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. There are two independent groups of eight cells in the grid, marked with a grey shading. Exactly four different digits can be found in each of the groups, each of them exactly twice.

title: Frame Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Numbers outside the grid are equal to the sum of the first 3 digits appearing in the corresponding row or column.

title: Frame-Diagonal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally, each marked diagonal must also contain each number from 1-9 exactly once. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the first 3 numbers placed in the corresponding direction.

title: Full or Half Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
A circle marked at the intersection of a 2×2 block of cells indicates that numbers placed in these cells are either all odd or all even. A square marked at the intersection of a 2×2 block of cells indicates that exactly two numbers placed in these cells are odd, and exactly two numbers are even.

title: Full Rank Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Full rows/columns (read left-right, right-left, top-bottom, bottom-top) form 36 distinct 9-digit numbers. Their rank from the lowest is given.

title: Futoshiki (Iso, Sudoku)
instruction:
Place a number from 1 to X (integers only) into each cell so that each number appears exactly once in each row and column. (X is the number of cells in each row.) Some inequality relations are given between adjacent cells; they must apply to the numbers in those cells.
Numbers may not repeat in any of the outlined regions.

title: Fuzzy Arrows Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Exactly one circle should be added on each grey line. One or two arrows should be drawn at all loose ends of each grey line then. Finally solve standard arrows sudoku. A number placed in a cell with a circle must be the sum of the numbers placed in cells the adjoining arrow passes through. Numbers may repeat on arrows.

title: German Whispers Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent digits along each marked line have a difference of at least 5.

title: Greater or Difference Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each number between two neighbouring cells is either the greater of the digits or the difference between the digits in those two cells.

title: Greater or Sum Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each number between two neighbouring cells is either the greater of the digits or the sum of the digits in those two cells.

title: Greater Sudoku
instruction: Number in a circle tells the greater one of the two adjacent numbers.

title: Greater than and Killer Sudoku
instruction:
Follow classic sudoku rules. There are several cages in the grid with a dashed-line outline. Sum of all digits in every cage is given.
Same digit cannot be repeated in one cage. All digits should follow given inequality signs.

title: Greater Than Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. All equality and inequality signs relate to the sums of killer cages. Some of the cages have no sum value attached. Digits must not repeat in cages.

title: GT Consecutive Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
Adjacent cells containing consecutive numbers are marked with inequalities. Numbers placed in these cells must satisfy the given inequality. Adjacent cells with no marking must not contain consecutive numbers.

title: Half Correct Quadruple Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. At some intersections of two crossing grid lines, a set of four digits is given. For each set, exactly 2 digits are correct (they appear at least once).

title: Half-Consecutive Palindrome Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Two of the gray lines are perfect palindromes and the other two gray lines are consecutive pair palindromes (i.e. paired palindrome positions must have digits which are consecutive to each other). Part of solving is to figure out which lines are perfect palindromes and which are consecutive pair palindromes.

title: Half-mosaic Sudoku
instruction: Fill in the grid with digits 1 to 9 so that every row, column and outlined box contains nine different digits. If there are two digits in one cell, the smaller one should be to the left from the higher one.

title: Halved Squares Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked region contains each digit exactly once. Some cells are halved by one of the diagonals. For any such cell, exactly one of its two halves shall contain a digit. That digit is considered to belong to the row and column containing its cell, as well as to the region connected to the half cell the digit is in.

title: Hashtag Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each of the four marked diagonals must also contain each digit from 1-9 exactly once.

title: Hidden Clone Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The shaded region must be at least once cloned somewhere else in the grid, without rotation or reflection and the digits in the corresponding cells must be equal. The clone may overlap partially with the original shaded region, but not completely. Digits may repeat in a shaded region.

title: Hit the Target Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. There must be a path from 1 in the arrow to 9 in the target via the digits 2 to 8 so that 2 must be placed in a cell in the direction of the arrow. Consider the cell with 2 a new arrow cell but with the arrow turned 90 degrees to the right. Now 3 must be in a cell in that direction. Consider the cell with 3 a new arrow but with the arrow turned another 90 degrees to the right. Now 4 must be in a cell in that direction ... and so on until you hit the target.

title: Inclusion Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each digit at the intersection of four cells must be present in at least one of those four cells. If a digit is repeated in the clue it must appear twice in the surrounding cells.

title: Inequality Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If there is an inequality relation marked between two cells then the digits placed in the cells should obey the relation.

title: Inequality Triples Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. For each pair of adjacent boxes, the inequality relationship between three pairs of numbers across the box boundary is the same.

title: Inner Frame Sum Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Numbers outside the grid represents sum of 2nd, 3rd and 4th digit in the corresponding direction.

title: Interconnected Classics Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Grey cells contain the same digits as the corresponding grey cells in the other puzzle.

title: Irregular Alternating Stripes Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 9-cell region contains each digit exactly once. Along each grey stripe, digits must be alternately larger and smaller than their immediate neighbours. Digits may not repeat anywhere on a stripe.

title: Irregular Builder Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked contains each digit exactly once. Some region boundaries are missing, but all given borders must separate cells in different reqions.

title: Irregular dots Sudoku
instruction: Follow irrregular sudoku rules. Rows and columns are marked with numbers 1 to 9. There is a white dot between two cells if the difference of the digits they contain is equal to the number of a row they are part of. There is a black dot between two cells if the sum of the digits they contain is equal to the number of a row they are part of. The same applies to the columns. All possible dots are drawn.

title: Irregular Lines Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 9-cell region contains each digit exactly once. Some regions are connected by lines.

title: Irregular math sudoku
instruction:
Place numbers from 1-8 in the grid so that each number appears exactly once in each row, column and outlined region.
The numbers on the right and left show the sum of the first 2 numbers in the row from the given side. The numbers on top and bottom
show the product of the first 2 numbers in the column from the given side.

title: Irregular Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 9-cell region contains each digit exactly once.

title: Italian Job Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, as in Diagonal Sudoku, each digit appears exactly once in the two main diagonals; as in Killer sudoku, the sum of the digits in each cage must equal the value given in the upper-left corner of that cage. Digits cannot repeat inside a cage.
(Note: Some cages will have cells connected only diagonally.)

title: Japanese Sums Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1 to 9 into each of the empty cells so that each digit appears exactly once in each row, column and 3x3 outlined box. Shade some cells in the grid such that the numbers outside the grid represent sums of digits in white cells of the corresponding row or column. If there is more than one sum, the given order is valid and there must be at least one shaded cell between the sums. Some shaded cells may be given.

title: Jigsaw Killer Sudoku
instruction: Follow irregular sudoku rules. There are several cages in the grid with a dashed-line outline. (Several of them are formed by two or three diagonally connected cells.) Sum of all digits in every cage is given. Same digit cannot be repeated in one cage.

title: Killer 007 Sudoku
instruction: Fill in the grid with digits 0 to 7 so that every row, column and boldly outlined region contains two zeros and all remaining digits exactly once. Two cells with a zero cannot share an edge. No digit can be repeated in a region with a dashed border. The sum of all digits in every region with a dashed border is given.

title: Killer Hidden Arrows Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits places in each marked cage must sum to the total given in its top-left corner. Digits must not repeat in cages. All cages contain a standard arrow clue, which is hidden. The circle part of the arrow will be at one end of the cage with the arrow extending along the cage to the other end. The arrow must fill the entire cage. The circle may enclose any number of digits, and multi-digit totals are read in the direction of the arrow.

title: Killer Little Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits placed in each marked cage must sum to the total given in its top-left. Digits must not repeat in cages. Numbers along the arrows indicate the sum of the digits in the corresponding direction.

title: Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits placed in each marked cage must sum to the total given in its top-left. Digits must not repeat in cages.

title: Knightmare Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Two cells which are a knight's move apart must not contain digits which add to either 5 or 15.

title: Kropki Pairs Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent cells containing digits whose difference is 1 are marked with a white circle. Adjacent cells containing digits whose ratio is 2 are marked with a black circle. A circle between between a 1 and a 2 can be either white or black.
Not all dots are given. In other words, adjacent cells with no marking may contain digits either whose difference is 1 or whose ratio is 2.

title: Kropki Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent cells containing digits whose difference is 1 are marked with a white circle. Adjacent cells containing digits whose ratio is 2 are marked with a black circle. A circle between between a 1 and a 2 can be either white or black. Adjacent cells with no marking must not contain digits either whose difference is 1 or whose ratio is 2.

title: LC Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Whenever two adjacent numbers in a row or column add up to 50 or 100, this is marked by a letter L or C respectively, placed on the line separating the two numbers. Two-digit numbers are formed by combining adjacent digits (reading from left to right or top to bottom only). All possible letters L and C are marked. Row and column strings which would result in the leters L and C coinciding (e.g. 5248) will not occur.

title: Letter Sudoku
instruction: Place a letter in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 3x3 box contains the same set of letters. There are no repeated letters in the set.

title: Liar Diagonal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. One diagonal must contain each digit from 1-9 exactly once, whilst the other diagonal must have one or more digits repeated. It is part of solving to determine which diagonal has the corresponding property.

title: Liar Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits placed in all but one marked cage must sum to the total given in its top-left. Exactly one marked cage displays a wrong sum. Digits must not repeat in cages.

title: Liar Odd/Even Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in circles must be odd and digits in squares must be even except for exactly one cell which has the wrong symbol.

title: Liar Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
Exactly one given clue in each row, column and marked 3 × 3 box is incorrect.

title: Liar Zones Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each clue in the top-left corner of a marked cage displays two digits correctly and one incorrectly. Digits may repeat in cages.

title: Little Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the digits contained in the cells in the direction of the corresponding arrow.

title: Lonely Number Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. There are 80 numbers in the grid that fulfill a following condition: each of these happy numbers has a consecutive friend in a cell sharing an edge with it. There is exactly one lonely number in the grid that doesn't have a consecutive friend next to it.

title: Magic Squares Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Three of the given 3x3 blocks are magic squares, which means that the sum of the digits on both diagonals, the three horizontal lines of three cells and the three vertical lines of three cells is the same.

title: Mastermind Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The grey central box serves as an exemplar. The four corner boxes are compared to the central one and the result of comparison is given in the style of a Mastermind puzzle for every triplet of cells. A black dot is given for every identical digit in the same position. A white dot is given for an identical digit but not in the same position. A cross means that there is nothing in common in this triplet.

title: Mathrax Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some intersections are marked by a number and an operator (+, -, x, /) in a circle. The number is the result of the operation, applied to both pairs of diagonally opposite cells. an "E" or "O" in the circle indicates that all four adjacent digits are even or odd respectively.

title: Matryoshka Sudoku
instruction: Apply Classic Sudoku rules. Also some areas are overlapped, with sizes from 6×6 to 9×9.

title: Max Ascending Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the length of the longest series of ascending digits in the corresponding direction.

title: Meandering Diagonals Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. In each set of cells connected by meandering diagonal lines each digit appears at most once.

title: Median Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The given digits outside the grid have to be placed in one of the first three cells from that side. Of the other two digits in the first three cells, one digit is smaller and one digit is greater than the given digit.

title: Min-Max Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each number outside the grid is the sum of the highest and lowest digit in the first three cells in the corresponding row or column.

title: Minesweeper (Sudoku)
instruction:
Place mines into the empty cells in the grid, at most one mine per cell, so that each number in a cell represents the number of mines adjacent to that cell (including diagonally adjacent cells).
Each row, column, and outlined region in the grid must contain exactly 3 mines.

title: Missing Digit Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 0-9 in each empty cell. Digits in a row, column and 3x3 box must be different. The clues outside the grid indicate the digit that is not included in the corresponding row/column.

title: Morse Numbers Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The combination of odd and even digits along an arrow corresponds to the "Morse-like coding" for the digit in the circled cell which follows.

title: Multi Diagonal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Every marked diagonal line contains each digit no more than once.

title: Multi Sudoku
instruction: Place a number from 1-9 in each empty cell in the overlapping grids such that each row, column and marked 3×3 box contains each number exactly once.

title: Multiples Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some numbers are given on the edges between two adjacent cells. Treating those cells as a two-digit number (reading left to right or top to bottom), the two-digit number must be a multiple of the clue. (For example, near a 7 clue, the two digits can be 14, 21, 28, 35,...)

title: Multiplication Table Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in the second row of the cage (read from left to right) indicate the product of the digits in the first row.

title: Neighbor Sums Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, some edges are marked with the sums of the digits in the adjacent cells.

title: Next to 9 Sudoku
instruction: Apply Classic Sudoku rules. Additionally, clues outside the grid indicate the digits that sit next to 9 in that row or column. These clues are given in increasing order.

title: Nine Pins Sudoku
instruction: Apply class sudoku rules. For each number 1-9, there must be at least one set of three identical digits in a straight line of adjacent cells somewhere in the grid.

title: No 3 Consecutive Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, no group of three contiguous cells within any row or column can contain a set of consecutive digits in any order. (For example, the grid cannot have 345, 354, 435, 453, 534, or 543 in three contiguous cells).

title: No Even Neighbours Sudoku
instruction: Apply standard Sudoku rules. No two cells containing even digits can share an edge.

title: No Touch Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Equal digits must not touch each other diagonally.

title: Non-Consecutive Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits placed in adjacent cells must not be consecutive.

title: Nonconsecutive spiral Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. (The classic sudoku regions are marked with the shading not by the bold lines.) Pair of adjacent cells following the spiral way marked by a bold line cannot contain consecutive digits.

title: Number 5 is Alive Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits must not repeat in cages and their sums end with 5 (the sums can be 5, 15, 25, 35 or 45).

title: Numbered Rooms Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the digit which has to be placed in the Nth cell in the corresponding direction, where N is the digit placed in the first cell in that direction.

title: Odd even and killer Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Sum of the two digits in marked cages is given. Every pair of blank cells (= cells outside marked cages) sharing an edge have different parity.

title: Odd Even Bridge Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some circled cells are connected by a bridge. An odd digit in a circle denotes the number of odd digits on the bridge. An even digit in a circle equals the number of even digits on the bridge. The digits on the circles are not counted. If a circled cell is connected by more than one bridge, an odd/even digit in the cell denote the number of odd/even digits on each connected bridge (not the sum of all connected bridges).

title: Odd Even Count Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. An even digit placed in a cell with a circle indicates the number of even digits placed in the surrounding 8 cells. An odd digit placed in a cell with a circle indicates the number of odd digits placed in the surrounding cells. Numbers placed in cells without a circle may have either of these properties.

title: Odd Even Sum Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Moreover, the sum of two digits in every cage marked with “O” is odd and the sum of two digits in every cage marked with “E” is even.

title: Odd Labyrinth Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally, there must be at least one path from the top left cell to the bottom right cell which passes orthogonally through only odd digits.

title: Odd Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Numbers placed in shaded cells must be odd.

title: Odd Sums Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
The clues outside the grid indicate, in the correct order, the sums of all consecutive sequences of odd numbers in the corresponding direction. There must be at least one even number placed between two such sequences.

title: Odd Tapa Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. All odd digits must be orthogonally interconnected with no 2x2 area covered completely by odd digits (like shaded cells in Tapa puzzle).

title: Odd-Even Pairs Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Exactly one odd and one even digit must be placed in each marked pair of adjacent cells.

title: Odd-Even-Big-Small Sudoku
instruction:
Place a digit from 1-8 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 8-cell box contains each number exactly once. The clues outside the grid indicate that the first two cells in the corresponding direction must contain digits matching the following description:
O = Odd digits {1, 3, 5, 7}; E = Even digits {2, 4, 6, 8}; B = Big digits {5, 6, 7, 8}; S = Small digits {1, 2, 3, 4}.

title: Odd/Even Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in circles must be odd and digits in squares must be even.

title: Offset Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A digit in a grey cell must be placed in the Nth position in the next row, where N is the digit immediately to the right of the grey cell.

title: One Knight Step Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some given digits are shaded. All such digits have exactly one digit of the same value one knight step away from its own position.

title: One or Two Difference Pairs Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If a circle is given between two adjacent cells, then the difference in those cells must be 1 or 2. Not all circles are given; adjacent cells without a circle may contain either consecutive digits, digits whose difference is 2 or other non-consecutive digits.

title: One Touch Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Every digit 1-9 has exactly one touching pair in the grid. All these touches are marked by a circle. If there are less than 9 circles in the grid, that means there is at least one marked 2x2 block which contains two pairs of touching digits.

title: One-five-nine Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Digits in the first column say in which column you can find number 1 in the respective row. Digits in the fifth column say in which column you can find number 5. Digits in the nineth column say in which column you can find number 9.

title: Online Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The set of given digits on each grey line must appear on the same line in the solution. They may or may not appear in the same order.

title: Ordering Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. There is a set of 16 different two-digit numbers in the marked cages. Their order from the lowest to the highest is given by small numbers from 1 to 16.

title: Orthogonal spacing Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Let’s denote D a number in the marked cell. Let’s denote Aand B the two neighbouring numbers in the cells marked by arrows. The distance between numbers A and B in an ortogonal row / column (marked by the short grey line) should be equal to D. (It is possible to have two signs in one cell.)

title: Outside 234 Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits outside the grid must appear in the 2nd, 3rd or 4th cell of the grid in the corresponding direction.

title: Outside consecutive Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Numbers outside the grid tells the count of consecutive adjacent pairs in the particular row / column.

title: Outside Greater Than Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The signs outside the grid indicate how many inequality signs should be placed between neighbouring cells in the corresponding direction.

title: Outside Killer Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, the clues outside the grid indicate the sum of at least one pair of neighboring cells in that row or column.

title: Outside Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits outside the grid must appear within the first three cells in the corresponding direction.

title: Overlapping Irregular Sudoku
instruction: Place the digits from 1 to 9 in every row and column of both overlapping grids and in every bold outlined area for the puzzle as a whole.

title: Overlapping Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally two grids are overlapping.

title: Paint It Black Sudoku
instruction: Shade some cells such that the numbers outside the grid indicate the number of shaded cells in each shaded stretch in the corresponding row or column. If there is more than one number, the given order is valid and there must be at least one white cell between the stretches of shaded cells. Then transfer the digits from the white cells into the blank grid and solve it as classic Sudoku. Place a digit from 1 to 9 into each of the empty cells so that each digit appears exactly once in each row, column and 3x3 outlined box.

title: Palindrome Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits along any grey line form a palindrome i.e. they read the same in both directions.

title: Pandigital Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules.
The numbers placed in each shaded region must form the given equations when they are read as the corresponding n-digit numbers.

title: Parity circles Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. If there is an odd number O in a circle, there are exactly O odd numbers around it. If there is an even number E in a circle, there are exactly E even numbers around it. All possible circles are given.

title: Parity Skyscrapers Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each digit represents the height of a building. The clues outside the grid indicate whether an odd (O) or even (E) number of buildings is visible from the corresponding direction. A taller building will hide any shorter buildings behind it.

title: Parity Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits along any grey line have the same parity (all odd or all even).

title: Parquet Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules, except some cells are larger than others and belong in multiple rows and/or columns. Each digit appears exactly once in each of the following regions: the 12 rows, the 12 columns, and the nine outlined 4x4 regions.

title: Partitioned Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the digit(s) in the respective row or column, when the row or column is partitioned at certain points (not given), in the correct order. Clues are either given for the entire row or column, or not given at all.

title: Perfect Squares Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If two horizontally or vertically adjacent cells (read from left to right and from top to bottom) form a two-digit perfect square, it is marked by a gray square. All two-digit perfect squares are marked.

title: Pinocchio Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Three digits are written in a different layout. Two of these digits are correct, while the third one is incorrect.

title: Placement Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Place the given fragments inside the grid without rotating or mirroring. Fragments can touch each other, but can not overlap each other or given digits.

title: Plus minus killer Sudoku
instruction: Follow irregular sudoku rules (digits 1 to 7). No digit can be repeated in a region with a dashed border. The sum of all digits in every region with a dashed border is given while digits in grey cells are counted as negative ones.

title: Plus minus lines Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Cell with a horizontal line contains a sum of the two neighbouring cells in the particular row. Cell with a vertical line contains a difference of the two neighbouring cells in the particular column. All possible lines are given.

title: Plus or Minus Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A grey horizontal or vertical bar symbol in a cell indicates that the digit in the cell is equal to the sum or difference of its horizontal or vertical neighbours. Not all possible bars are marked; cells without a bar may or may not indicate either sum or difference of its horizontal or vertical neighbours.

title: Point to Next Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If a digit N is placed in a cell containing an arrow, then the digit N+1 must be placed in a cell pointed at by the arrow. Not all possible arrows are given.

title: Pointing Differents Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Numbers outside the grid indicate the number of different numbers placed in the cells in the corresponding diagonal direction.

title: Pointing Digits Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A digit N in a cell with an arrow sees another digit N, which is N cells away in the arrow's direction (diagonally). Arrows may be reciprocal (pointing towards each other). Not all possible arrows are given.

title: Position Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Numbers outside the grid signify the position of the highest of the nearest three digits in the corresponding row/column.

title: Position Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. External clues give information relating to the first two digits (called A and B) in the corresponding row or column. Clues next to the grid give the sum of A and B. Clues in the grey band further outside the grid give the sum of the digits in positions A and B.

title: Prague star Sudoku
instruction: Place digits from 1 to 9, they don't repeat in marked triangles and rows of cells in three principal directions (of any length, e.g.4+5).

title: Prime Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A dot means that the sum of the digits in the two neighbouring cells is a prime number. All possible dots are given.

title: Product Arrow Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, some arrows are in the grid. The encircled 1, 2, or more digits must be equal to the product of the digits that appear along the path of the arrow. Digits can repeat within an arrow shape.

title: Product last digit Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Number in a circle is equal to the last digit of a product of all digits along the corresponding arrow.

title: Product Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A number given on the border between two diagonally adjacent cells is the product of the digits in the two indicated cells.

title: Pyramidal Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, there are some pyramids in the grid made out of grey cells. For each row above the base of the pyramid, each grey cell must equal either the sum or the difference of the two grey cells diagonally beneath it.

title: Quad Max Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. An arrow on a grid point points to the highest digit in the four cells around it. Digits may repeat around an arrow, but the highest digit is always unique.

title: Quad Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A circle at a corner implies that one digit is the sum of the remaining three digits at that 2x2 square. Not all possible dots are given, i.e. there may or may not be additional 2x2 squares where one digit is the sum of the remaining three digits.

title: Quadro Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. No 2x2 square can only contain odd or even digits.

title: Quadruple Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. At some intersections of two crossing grid lines, a set of four digits is given. These digits must be placed in the four adjacent cells.

title: Queen Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. All digits 9 represent chess Queens. These Queens can not attack each other.

title: Rank Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A digit 'X' in a circle means that the digit in the cell is the X-th smallest number in the corresponding cage. Digits cannot repeat within a cage.

title: Ratio Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits placed in adjacent cells must satisfy the given ratios.

title: Read the Room Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules. Within each 3x3 region, exactly one of two constraints (A or B) applies:
(A) Digits places in adjacent cells within the 3x3 region must not be consecutive,
(B) Each cell must be adjacent to at least one other cell within the 3x3 region where the digits places in both are consecutive.
Neither constraint applies to adjacent cells in different 3x3 regions.

title: Region Sum Lines Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. For each line, digits on the line have an equal sum N within each box it passes through. If a line passes through the same box more than once, each individual segment of such a line within that box sums to N separately.

title: Remote Clones Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Lines in a cell mark that in those two directions both digits are the same at a distance of N where N is the value of that cell. Not all possible lines are given.

title: Renban Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each outlined cage must contain a set of distinct digits with the specified digit sum. All remaining squares form contiguous blocks of shaded cells and each block must contain a set of consecutive digits.

title: Renban Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Shaded regions must contain a set of distinct, consecutive digits in any order.
otherNames: Renban Group Sudoku, Renban Groups Sudoku

title: Repeated Neighbors Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, all cells having the same digit(s) more than once as orthogonal neighbors are shaded.
(If a cell is not shaded, it cannot have any digits repeated as orthogonal neighbors.)

title: Roman Numeral Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Instead of clues for the digits 1 to 9, this sudoku gives clues for Roman numerals I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, IX. Not all clues are complete numbers; some of them may be just part of the Roman numeral.

title: Rossini Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The arrows outside the grid indicate that the first three digits are in ascending or descending order (the highest digit is at the point of the arrow). If there is no arrow outside, the first three digits cannot be in either ascending or descending order.

title: Round Off Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each cage clue indicates the value of rounding off the two-digit number within that cage to the nearest multiple of 10. Numbers ending in digits 1-4 are rounded down, while numbers ending in digits 5-9 are rounded up.

title: Same sum Sudoku
instruction: The sum of four cells adjacent to a grey one is the same in the whole grid.

title: Scattered Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column, marked 9-cell region and nine shaded cells contains each digit exactly once.

title: Scattered X-Sums Sudoku
instruction: Place the digits from 1 to 9 in every row, column, bold outlined area and the nine grey cells. Clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the first X digits in the corresponding direction. X is the first digit in the corresponding direction.

title: Search Closest Nine Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some arrows are marked in the grid. Each arrow points to the digit 9 in the respective row, column or diagonal. The digit in the cell with the arrow is the distance from this cell to the corresponding digit 9. For each diagonally oriented arrow, the digit in the cell with the arrow shows the distance to the closest digit 9 in that direction.

title: Search Nine Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Some arrows are marked in the grid. Each arrow points to the digit 9 in the respective row or column. The digit in the cell with the arrow is the distance from this cell to the corresponding digit 9.

title: Sequences Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits along each marked line have to be different and must form an arithmetic sequence in the correct order. Two straight lines may cross each other.

title: Serbian Frame Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The clues outside the rows indicate the sum of the digits to be placed in the 2nd and 3rd cells in the corresponding direction. The clues outside the columns indicate the sums of the digits to be placed in the 3rd and 4th cells in the corresponding direction.

title: Serbian Irregular Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1 to 9 in each empty cell so that each digit appears exactly once in every row and column. In bold regions of size N ≤ 9, the digits 1 to N must appear exactly once. In bold regions of size N > 9, the (N-9) largest digits will appear twice while the remaining digits will appear once. For example, a region with 11 cells will contain the digits 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 8, 9, 9, with the two largest digits, 8 and 9, repeated.

title: Set Double Block Sudoku
instruction: Blacken exactly two cells in every row, column and outlined box. Fill in the remaining blank cells with digits from a given set so that no digit is repeated in a row, column or outlined box. The sum of all digits between the two black cells is given for some rows and columns.

title: Shaken Clones Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each shape is cloned, including the digits it contains. Position of digits within the shape may change. A cloned shape may undergo rotation and/or reflection. Digits may repeat in a shape.

title: Shape Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each shaded shape below the grid must replace one of the shaded regions in the grid. Shapes can be rotated, but cannot be reflected.

title: Single Parity Skyscrapers Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each digit represents the height of a building. The clues outside the grid indicate either the number of even-heighted buildings or number of odd-heighted buildings visible from the corresponding direction, or both. A taller building will hide any shorter buildings behind it.

title: Skyscrapers on arrows Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. Digits along the arrows represent buildings of the height given by its value. Number in a circle tells you the count of visible buildings along the corresponding arrow, in the direction from the circle to the opposite end of the arrow. Higher building covers all smaller or equal building behind it.

title: Skyscrapers Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each digit represents the height of a building. The clues outside the grid indicate the number of buildings visible from the corresponding direction. A taller building will hide any shorter buildings behind it.

title: Slalom Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, in each row the cells between two poles must contain a set of consecutive digits.

title: Small Difference Loop Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. One or more loops of orthogonally connected cells will be shaded grey. Along such a loop, each pair of adjacent digits must have a difference of either 1 or 2.

title: Snowflake Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-6 in each empty cell so that each digit appears exactly once in each “row“ and outlined hexagon.

title: Somewhere Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, in each cage, at least one cell has the indicated digit.

title: Spiral Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally, exactly three of the nine 3x3 boxes have the property that their eight perimeter cells are in a strictly increasing order with starting cell and direction unspecified. The 3 sprial boxes, and the starting cell/direction in these boxes need to be identified as part of solving.

title: Star Product Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, the clues outside the grid indicate the product of the digits placed in the cells containing stars in the corresponding row or column.

title: Step by step Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. There is exactly one way how to go in a cycle through all grey arrows, every time making that number of steps to the next arrow which is given by the digit in the previous cell with an arrow

title: Stretched Thermo Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Starting at the "bulb", digits placed along each marked thermometer cannot decrease (they can increase or stay equal).

title: Subsets Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. For every pair of connected cages, the set of digits in the smaller cage must be a subset of the set of digits in the larger cage. Digits on the corresponding connecting line must belong to the set of digits in the larger cage but must not belong to the subset of digits in the smaller cage. Digits are not allowed to repeat in any cage.

title: Substitution Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. A cell with a letter must contain a digit whose mapped word contains the corresponding letter.

title: Sudoku
instruction: Place a digit from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 3x3 box contains each digit exactly once.

title: Sudoku (Battleships)
instruction:
Locate the indicated fleet of ships in the grid. Ships may be rotated before being placed in the grid. Each piece of a ship occupies a single cell. A cell that does not contain a ship piece is considered "sea". Ships do not touch each other, not even diagonally (that is, if two ship pieces are in cells that share an edge or a corner, they must be part of the same ship). The contents of some cells are given for you (wavy lines indicate "sea").
Each number to the top or left of the grid reveals the number of ship pieces that must be located in that row or column (including any that might be given for you).
Also place digits from 1-6 into all cells without digits such that each row, column and marked 2×3 box contains each digit exactly once. (Ignore the numbers outside the grid for this purpose.) The indicated fleet of ships may have digits on ship pieces; these digits must match the digits placed in the grid for the corresponding ships.

title: Sudoku N
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. If any operation (+, -, x, /) performed between two orthogonally adjacent cells leads to a result of N, then the two cells are separated by a grey circle. If there is no grey circle, the result can not be N. The number N can be any integer in the range from 1-9. N is not given. It is part of solving to determine N.

title: Sudoku with Stars
instruction: Place either a digit from 1 to 7 or a star in each empty cell, so that each digit appears exactly once in each row, column and outlined region. There must be exactly two stars in in each row, column and outlined region. Stars cannot be placed in adjacent cells that touch even at a corner.

title: Sudokuro
instruction: Place a digit from 1 to 9 into each of the empty cells so that each digit appears exactly once in each row, column and irregular shaped outlined box. The sum of digits in each horizontal and vertical group of cells is given on its left and top respectively.

title: Sum 10 Twin Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The sum of the numbers placed in corresponding cells in two regions of the same shape must equal 10.

title: Sum 100 Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The sum of numbers in the shaded cells in each row is exactly 100. Two adjacent shaded cells in the same row form a two-digit number.

title: Sum 5 to 15 Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. There are 22 outlined cages in the grid that have sums between 5 and 15. Every sum is used exactly twice and the combinations of digits never repeat. Some of the sums are already given. Digits may repeat in a cage.

title: Sum by X Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each number outside the grid is the sum of the first few digits from the edge. The nearest shaded cell in the corresponding direction indicates the number of digits included in the sum.

title: Sum it up Sudoku
instruction: Follow diagonal sudoku rules. Each row and both diagonals can be interpreted as a sequence of three numbers, a 4-digit, 3-digit (in grey cells) and 2-digit one. The total of those numbers is given.

title: Sum Next to Nine Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Clues outside the grid indicate the sum of the immediate neighbours of the digit 9 in the corresponding row or column.

title: Sum Nine Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Adjacent cells containing numbers with the sum 9 are marked with a rhombus. Adjacent cells with no marking must not contain numbers with the sum 9.

title: Sum or Product Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The number in each cage shows either the sum or the product (or both) of all digits inside the cage. Digits may be repeated within a cage.

title: Sum Sandwich Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Additionally, the clues outside the grid indicate all numbers placed in the corresponding direction that are the sum of the numbers placed in the two neighbouring cells in that direction, e.g. 132. If a number is not clued outside the grid, then it must not be the sum of its two neighbours in that direction.

title: Sum Set Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The sum of the digits in each marked cage must be different.

title: Sum Skyscrapers Sudoku
instruction: Place a number from 1-9 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row, column and marked 3×3 box contains each number exactly once. Each number represents the height of a building. The clues outside the grid indicate the sum of buildings visible from the corresponding direction. A taller building will hide any shorter buildings behind it.

title: Superconsecutive Sudoku
instruction: Follow classic sudoku rules. There is one circled number N in every box. If any number of that particular box has a neighbour sharing an edge which is differing exactly by N there is a dot between them. (All possible dots are marked.)

title: Surplus Sudoku
instruction: Place a number from 1-7 in each empty cell in the grid such that each row and column contains each number exactly once, and each marked 8-cell region contains each number at most once.

title: Sym-Sum Anti-Knight Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The contents of any two cells in symmetrical positions relative to the centre of the grid must sum to 10. Digits placed in cells connected by a chess Knight's move must be different.

title: Symmetric Product Sums Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Each rectangular region is of size 2xn and the number in the circle is the sum of the products of all 180-degree symmetric pairs of digits within the region. Digits may be repeated within a rectangular region.

title: Symmetric Unequal Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The digits placed in any pair of cells related by 180 degrees rotational symmetry (e.g. R1C2 and R9C8) must not repeat. A center of the symmetry is in the middle of the grid - cell in R5C5.

title: Tableaux Sudoku
instruction: Apply class sudoku rules. Within cages, digits must increase from top to bottom along columns and increase along rows from left to right, even if there is a gap between two cells in a cage. Digits within a cage must be distinct.

title: Ten/Eleven Sudoku
instruction:
Apply Classic Sudoku rules.
Additionally, if a gray bar is given between two adjacent cells, then the two digits in those cells sum to ten or eleven. If a gray bar is not given, the two digits cannot sum to ten or eleven.

title: Tens Position Products Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Number between a pair of adjacent cell indicates the number of the tens position of the product of the numbers placed in these cells (e.g. if 3 and 4 are placed in the cells, their product is 12 and the clue would be 1).

title: Thermo Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Starting at the "bulb", digits placed along each marked thermometer must form a strictly increasing sequence.

title: Three Lines Sudoku
instruction:
Apply classic sudoku rules. In each group of three lines:
- One will follow Arrow Sudoku rules. The digit placed in a cell with a circle must be the sum of the digits placed in the cells that the adjoining arrow passes through. Digits may repeat on arrows. It is up to the solver to determine which end has the circle.
- One will follow Thermo Sudoku rules. Starting at the “bulb”, digits placed along the thermometer must form a strictly increasing sequence. It is up to the solver to determine which end has the bulb.
- One will follow Palindrome Sudoku rules. Digits along the line read the same in both directions.
It is allowed that a line has properties of both Arrow and Thermo Sudoku (for instance: 1-2-3-6).

title: Three-Digit Numbers Killer Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. Digits in the gray strings represent three-digit numbers. All three-digit numbers in the cage must sum to the total given in its top-left corner. Digits must not repeat in cages.

title: Tic-Tac-Toe Sudoku
instruction: Apply classic sudoku rules. The central 3x3 region serves as a map to the 9 regions of the grid (for the example, its top-left cell corresponds to the top-left 3x3 region, and its central cell corresponds to the central 3x3 region). Wherever the central region contains an odd digit, the corresponding region must have three odd digits in at least one line (horizontal, vertical or diagonal). Wherever it contains an even digit, the corresponding region must have three even digits in a line. No region can contain both a line of odd digits and a line of even digits.

title: Tic-Tac-Toe Winner Sudoku
